data_work

General first data pull

source("setup.R")
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.2     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.1.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
## Loading required package: viridisLite
## 
## 
## Attaching package: 'plotly'
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     last_plot
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:graphics':
## 
##     layout
## 
## 
## 
## Attaching package: 'rstatix'
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter
## 
## 
## 
## Attaching package: 'httr'
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:plotly':
## 
##     config
## 
## 
## 
## Attaching package: 'jsonlite'
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     flatten
## 
## 
## 
## Attaching package: 'shinydashboard'
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:graphics':
## 
##     box
# Download the meta-data for all sites
meta_data <- snotel_info()

# View the first few rows to understand the structure
head(meta_data)
##   network state            site_name
## 1    SNTL    AK     elmendorf field 
## 2    SNTL    CO          alta lakes 
## 3    SNTL    MT          mill creek 
## 4    SNTL    UT           elk ridge 
## 5    SNTL    AK              hoonah 
## 6    SNTL    AK pilgrim hot springs 
##                                    description      start        end latitude
## 1             Outlet Ship Creek (190204010404) 2024-10-01 2026-02-10    61.25
## 2   South Fork San Miguel River (140300030103) 2025-09-01 2026-02-10    37.89
## 3              Upper Mill Creek (100700020302) 2025-07-01 2026-02-10    45.26
## 4              Cottonwood Creek (140802010402) 2024-10-01 2026-02-10    37.82
## 5                 Port Fredrick (190102110906) 2023-10-01 2026-02-10    58.12
## 6 Paystreak Creek-Pilgrim River (190501050702) 2024-07-01 2026-02-10    65.09
##   longitude elev        county site_id
## 1   -149.82   52     Anchorage    1332
## 2   -107.84 3441    San Miguel    1344
## 3   -110.41 2262          Park    1322
## 4   -109.77 2603      San Juan    1323
## 5   -135.41  463 Hoonah-angoon    1318
## 6   -164.92    6          Nome    1327
id = 438 # Deadman Hill = 438
# Download data for a specific site and return as a data frame
df <- snotel_download(site_id = id, internal = TRUE)
## Downloading site: deadman hill , with id: 438
# View the first few rows of the downloaded data
head(df)
##   network state     site_name
## 1    SNTL    CO deadman hill 
## 2    SNTL    CO deadman hill 
## 3    SNTL    CO deadman hill 
## 4    SNTL    CO deadman hill 
## 5    SNTL    CO deadman hill 
## 6    SNTL    CO deadman hill 
##                                                       description      start
## 1 Panhandle Creek-North Fork Cache La Poudre River (101900070502) 1978-10-01
## 2 Panhandle Creek-North Fork Cache La Poudre River (101900070502) 1978-10-01
## 3 Panhandle Creek-North Fork Cache La Poudre River (101900070502) 1978-10-01
## 4 Panhandle Creek-North Fork Cache La Poudre River (101900070502) 1978-10-01
## 5 Panhandle Creek-North Fork Cache La Poudre River (101900070502) 1978-10-01
## 6 Panhandle Creek-North Fork Cache La Poudre River (101900070502) 1978-10-01
##          end latitude longitude elev  county site_id       date
## 1 2026-02-10    40.81   -105.77 3121 Larimer     438 1978-10-01
## 2 2026-02-10    40.81   -105.77 3121 Larimer     438 1978-10-02
## 3 2026-02-10    40.81   -105.77 3121 Larimer     438 1978-10-03
## 4 2026-02-10    40.81   -105.77 3121 Larimer     438 1978-10-04
## 5 2026-02-10    40.81   -105.77 3121 Larimer     438 1978-10-05
## 6 2026-02-10    40.81   -105.77 3121 Larimer     438 1978-10-06
##   snow_water_equivalent snow_depth precipitation_cumulative temperature_max
## 1                     0         NA                     0.00              NA
## 2                     0         NA                     0.00              NA
## 3                     0         NA                     2.54              NA
## 4                     0         NA                     2.54              NA
## 5                     0         NA                     2.54              NA
## 6                     0         NA                     2.54              NA
##   temperature_min temperature_mean precipitation
## 1              NA               NA          0.00
## 2              NA               NA          2.54
## 3              NA               NA          0.00
## 4              NA               NA          0.00
## 5              NA               NA          0.00
## 6              NA               NA          0.00
# make this column date objects
df$date <- as.Date(df$date)

# Make columns for day of water year and water year
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    water_year_start = if_else(month(date) >= 10,
                               make_date(year(date), 10, 1),
                               make_date(year(date) - 1, 10, 1)),
    day_of_water_year = as.integer(date - water_year_start) + 1,
    water_year = if_else(month(date) >= 10,
                         year(date)+1,
                         year(date),
  )) %>%
  select(-water_year_start)

df %>% select(date, water_year, day_of_water_year) %>% head()
##         date water_year day_of_water_year
## 1 1978-10-01       1979                 1
## 2 1978-10-02       1979                 2
## 3 1978-10-03       1979                 3
## 4 1978-10-04       1979                 4
## 5 1978-10-05       1979                 5
## 6 1978-10-06       1979                 6
# view new columns
print(df$day_of_water_year)

## [17299] 132 133
# View a water year
library(ggplot2)
df_subset <- df %>% filter(water_year == 2025) # filter df to one year
ggplot(df_subset, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = snow_water_equivalent, color = "SWE")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = precipitation_cumulative, color = "Precipitation")) +
  labs(
    title = "Snow Water Equivalent and Precipitation – Water Year 2025",
    x = "Date",
    y = "mm of water",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Function to look at different time periods
compare_mean_swe_by_wyday <- function(
    df,
    swe_col = snow_water_equivalent,
    period1 = c(1980, 2000),
    period2 = c(2001, 2025)
) {
  
  df_proc <- df %>%
    mutate(
      period = case_when(
        water_year >= period1[1] & water_year <= period1[2] ~
          paste0(period1[1], "–", period1[2]),
        water_year >= period2[1] & water_year <= period2[2] ~
          paste0(period2[1], "–", period2[2]),
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(period))
  
  swe_mean <- df_proc %>%
    group_by(period, day_of_water_year) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_swe = mean({{ swe_col }}, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  ggplot(swe_mean, aes(x = day_of_water_year, y = mean_swe, color = period)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    labs(
      title = "Mean Snow Water Equivalent by Day of Water Year",
      x = "Day of Water Year",
      y = "Mean Snow Water Equivalent",
      color = "Period"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}

compare_mean_swe_by_wyday(df)

compare_mean_swe_by_wyday(df, swe_col=precipitation_cumulative)

Depth over years, starting in 2003-07-15

df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = snow_depth)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_date(
    limits = c(as.Date("2003-10-01"), NA)
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
## Warning: Removed 9131 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_line()`).

Computations for depth x SWE

# getting a better estimate than Mizukami
